2026-04-23T04:04:06.3796259Z ##[group]Run # Generate a secure random API key (360 bits of entropy, 40+ chars)
2026-04-23T04:04:06.3796803Z [36;1m# Generate a secure random API key (360 bits of entropy, 40+ chars)[0m
2026-04-23T04:04:06.3797229Z [36;1m# Mask immediately to prevent timing vulnerabilities[0m
2026-04-23T04:04:06.3797596Z [36;1mAPI_KEY=$(openssl rand -base64 45 | tr -d '/+=')[0m
2026-04-23T04:04:06.3797918Z [36;1mecho "::add-mask::${API_KEY}"[0m
2026-04-23T04:04:06.3798636Z [36;1m[0m
2026-04-23T04:04:06.3798807Z [36;1mPORT=3001[0m
2026-04-23T04:04:06.3798993Z [36;1m[0m
2026-04-23T04:04:06.3799167Z [36;1m# Set outputs for next steps[0m
2026-04-23T04:04:06.3799403Z [36;1m{[0m

2026-04-23T04:04:06.3800161Z [36;1m} >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"[0m

It added four resource types: `TrafficController`, `TrafficControllerFrontend`, `TrafficControllerAssociation`, and `ApplicationGatewayForContainersSecurityPolicy`. A README was included with one example (Traffic Controller), but several documentation gaps remain.\\n\\n## Gaps Found\\n\\n- **Missing example for `ApplicationGatewayForContainersSecurityPolicy`**: The CHANGELOG explicitly lists this resource as added, but the README contains no example or description of how to configure it (including WAF security policy via `WafSecurityPolicyId` / `WafSecurityPolicy` / `SecurityPolicyConfigurations`).\\n- **No dedicated `Samples/` test directory**: The snippet backing the README example lives in `tests/BasicServiceNetworkingTests.cs` rather than a `tests/Samples/Sample*.cs` file, which diverges from the repository convention for snippet-backed documentation.\\n- **Empty \\u0026#34;Next steps\\u0026#34; section**: The README\\u0026#39;s \\u0026#34;Next steps\\u0026#34; section has no content — it should link to related Azure Application Gateway for Containers documentation, `azd` deployment guides, and related provisioning packages.\\n\\n\\n\\u003cstrong\\u003e📐 Implementation Guide\\u003c/strong\\u003e\\n\\nThis section contains step-by-step instructions for a coding agent to implement the changes described above.\\n\\n### Step 1: Modify files\\n\\n**File: `sdk/servicenetworking/Azure.Provisioning.ServiceNetworking/README.md`** — Edit\\n\\nIn the `## Examples` section, add a new sub-section after the existing \\u0026#34;Create a Traffic Controller\\u0026#34; example:\\n\\n````markdown\\n### Configure a Security Policy (WAF)\\n\\nThis example demonstrates how to attach a WAF security policy to an Application Gateway for Containers Traffic Controller.\\n\\n```C# Snippet:ServiceNetworkingSecurityPolicy\\nInfrastructure infra = new();\\n\\nTrafficController controller =\\n    new(nameof(controller), TrafficController.ResourceVersions.V2025_01_01)\\n    {\\n        Tags = { [\\u0026#34;environment\\u0026#34;] = \\u0026#34;test\\u0026#34; },\\n    };\\ninfra.Add(controller);\\n\\nApplicationGatewayForContainersSecurityPolicy securityPolicy =\\n    new(nameof(securityPolicy), ApplicationGatewayForContainersSecurityPolicy.ResourceVersions.V2025_01_01)\\n    {\\n        Parent = controller,\\n        SecurityPolicyConfigurations = new SecurityPolicyConfigurations\\n        {\\n            WafSecurityPolicy = new WafSecurityPolicy\\n            {\\n                Id = new ResourceIdentifier(\\u0026#34;/subscriptions/00000000-0000-0000-0000-000000000000/resourceGroups/myRg/providers/Microsoft.Network/applicationGatewayWebApplicationFirewallPolicies/myWafPolicy\\u0026#34;),\\n            },\\n        },\\n    };\\ninfra.Add(securityPolicy);\\n```\\n````\\n\\nIn the `## Next steps` section, add:\\n\\n```markdown\\n## Next steps\\n\\n- Learn about [Azure Application Gateway for Containers]((learn.microsoft.com/redacted)\\n- Explore other `Azure.Provisioning.*` libraries to compose full infrastructure stacks\\n- Use [azd (Azure Developer CLI)]((learn.microsoft.com/redacted) to deploy your provisioned infrastructure\\n```\\n\\n---\\n\\n### Step 2: Add or update code snippets\\n\\nMove the existing snippet into a dedicated samples file and add the new security policy snippet.\\n\\n**Create file: `sdk/servicenetworking/Azure.Provisioning.ServiceNetworking/tests/Samples/Sample1_TrafficController.cs`**\\n\\n```csharp\\n// Copyright (c) Microsoft Corporation. All rights reserved.\\n// Licensed under the MIT License.\\n\\nusing System.Threading.Tasks;\\nusing Azure.Core;\\nusing Azure.Provisioning.Tests;\\nusing NUnit.Framework;\\n\\nnamespace Azure.Provisioning.ServiceNetworking.Tests.Samples;\\n\\npublic class Sample1_TrafficController\\n{\\n    [Test]\\n    public async Task CreateTrafficController()\\n    {\\n        await using Trycep test = new Trycep().Define(\\n            ctx =\\u0026gt;\\n            {\\n                #region Snippet:ServiceNetworkingBasic\\n                Infrastructure infra = new();\\n\\n                TrafficController controller =\\n                    new(nameof(controller), TrafficController.ResourceVersions.V2025_01_01)\\n                    {\\n                        Tags = { [\\u0026#34;environment\\u0026#34;] = \\u0026#34;test\\u0026#34; },\\n                    };\\n                infra.Add(controller);\\n\\n                TrafficControllerFrontend frontend =\\n                    new(nameof(frontend), TrafficControllerFrontend.ResourceVersions.V2025_01_01)\\n                    {\\n                        Parent = controller,\\n                    };\\n                infra.Add(frontend);\\n\\n                TrafficControllerAssociation association =\\n                    new(nameof(association), TrafficControllerAssociation.ResourceVersions.V2025_01_01)\\n                    {\\n                        Parent = controller,\\n                        AssociationType = TrafficControllerAssociationType.Subnets,\\n                        SubnetId = new ResourceIdentifier(\\u0026#34;/subscriptions/00000000-0000-0000-0000-000000000000/resourceGroups/myRg/providers/Microsoft.Network/virtualNetworks/myVnet/subnets/mySubnet\\u0026#34;),\\n                    };\\n                infra.Add(association);\\n                #endregion\\n\\n                return infra;\\n            });\\n        // Test assertion omitted from snippet\\n#if !SNIPPET\\n        test.Compare(\\n            \\u0026#34;\\u0026#34;\\u0026#34;\\n            `@description`(\\u0026#39;The location for the resource(s) to be deployed.\\u0026#39;)\\n            param location string = resourceGroup().location\\n\\n            resource controller \\u0026#39;Microsoft.ServiceNetworking/trafficControllers@2025-01-01\\u0026#39; = {\\n              name: take(\\u0026#39;controller-\\\\$\\\\{uniqueString(resourceGroup().id)}\\u0026#39;, 24)\\n              tags: {\\n                environment: \\u0026#39;test\\u0026#39;\\n              }\\n              location: location\\n            }\\n\\n            resource frontend \\u0026#39;Microsoft.ServiceNetworking/trafficControllers/frontends@2025-01-01\\u0026#39; = {\\n              name: take(\\u0026#39;frontend-\\\\$\\\\{uniqueString(resourceGroup().id)}\\u0026#39;, 24)\\n              location: location\\n              parent: controller\\n            }\\n\\n            resource association \\u0026#39;Microsoft.ServiceNetworking/trafficControllers/associations@2025-01-01\\u0026#39; = {\\n              name: take(\\u0026#39;association-\\\\$\\\\{uniqueString(resourceGroup().id)}\\u0026#39;, 24)\\n              location: location\\n              properties: {\\n                associationType: \\u0026#39;subnets\\u0026#39;\\n                subnet: {\\n                  id: \\u0026#39;/subscriptions/00000000-0000-0000-0000-000000000000/resourceGroups/myRg/providers/Microsoft.Network/virtualNetworks/myVnet/subnets/mySubnet\\u0026#39;\\n                }\\n              }\\n              parent: controller\\n            }\\n            \\u0026#34;\\u0026#34;\\u0026#34;);\\n#endif\\n    }\\n\\n    [Test]\\n    public async Task ConfigureSecurityPolicy()\\n    {\\n        await using Trycep test = new Trycep().Define(\\n            ctx =\\u0026gt;\\n            {\\n                #region Snippet:ServiceNetworkingSecurityPolicy\\n                Infrastructure infra = new();\\n\\n                TrafficController controller =\\n                    new(nameof(controller), TrafficController.ResourceVersions.V2025_01_01)\\n                    {\\n                        Tags = { [\\u0026#34;environment\\u0026#34;] = \\u0026#34;test\\u0026#34; },\\n                    };\\n                infra.Add(controller);\\n\\n                ApplicationGatewayForContainersSecurityPolicy securityPolicy =\\n                    new(nameof(securityPolicy), ApplicationGatewayForContainersSecurityPolicy.ResourceVersions.V2025_01_01)\\n                    {\\n                        Parent = controller,\\n                        SecurityPolicyConfigurations = new SecurityPolicyConfigurations\\n                        {\\n                            WafSecurityPolicy = new WafSecurityPolicy\\n                            {\\n                                Id = new ResourceIdentifier(\\u0026#34;/subscriptions/00000000-0000-0000-0000-000000000000/resourceGroups/myRg/providers/Microsoft.Network/applicationGatewayWebApplicationFirewallPolicies/myWafPolicy\\u0026#34;),\\n                            },\\n                        },\\n                    };\\n                infra.Add(securityPolicy);\\n                #endregion\\n\\n                return infra;\\n            });\\n#if !SNIPPET\\n        // Bicep output assertion would go here\\n        await Task.CompletedTask;\\n#endif\\n    }\\n}\\n```\\n\\n**Snippet mappings:**\\n\\n| Snippet name | Test file | README section |\\n|---|---|---|\\n| `ServiceNetworkingBasic` | `tests/Samples/Sample1_TrafficController.cs` | `## Examples \\u0026gt; It added four resource types: `TrafficController`, `TrafficControllerFrontend`, `TrafficControllerAssociation`, and `ApplicationGatewayForContainersSecurityPolicy`. A README was included with one example (Traffic Controller), but several documentation gaps remain.\\n\\n## Gaps Found\\n\\n- **Missing example for `ApplicationGatewayForContainersSecurityPolicy`**: The CHANGELOG explicitly lists this resource as added, but the README contains no example or description of how to configure it (including WAF security policy via `WafSecurityPolicyId` / `WafSecurityPolicy` / `SecurityPolicyConfigurations`).\\n- **No dedicated `Samples/` test directory**: The snippet backing the README example lives in `tests/BasicServiceNetworkingTests.cs` rather than a `tests/Samples/Sample*.cs` file, which diverges from the repository convention for snippet-backed documentation.\\n- **Empty \\u0026#34;Next steps\\u0026#34; section**: The README\\u0026#39;s \\u0026#34;Next steps\\u0026#34; section has no content — it should link to related Azure Application Gateway for Containers documentation, `azd` deployment guides, and related provisioning packages.\\n\\n\\n\\u003cstrong\\u003e📐 Implementation Guide\\u003c/strong\\u003e\\n\\nThis section contains step-by-step instructions for a coding agent to implement the changes described above.\\n\\n### Step 1: Modify files\\n\\n**File: `sdk/servicenetworking/Azure.Provisioning.ServiceNetworking/README.md`** — Edit\\n\\nIn the `## Examples` section, add a new sub-section after the existing \\u0026#34;Create a Traffic Controller\\u0026#34;
